instruction: CMS-8200 HSS 技术描述文档的用途和读者对象是什么？
output: This document provides an overview of the CMS-8200 HSS product from a software and hardware perspective. It describes the product features, management tools, standards support, statistics, service and support, and product specifications. The Nokia CMS-8200 HSS Technical Description is intended for engineers or technicians who require an overview of the CMS-8200 HSS product. Prerequisites include good knowledge of modern communications systems, mobile end-user devices, digital network technologies and protocols including SIP/SDP, ability to read IMS System Description, and general knowledge on database concepts.

instruction: IMS 是什么？HSS 在 IMS 中的角色是什么？
output: The IP Multimedia Subsystem (IMS) defined by 3GPP is a generic architecture that provides multimedia and voice over IP services. The IMS enables convergence of and access to real time services, streaming, messaging, data and web-based technologies. Home subscriber server (HSS) is embodied by the product Nokia CMS-8200 HSS, which provides subscriber data storage and handling, as well as support for authentication, routing, and subscriber administration.

instruction: Nokia HSS 架构有哪些层次和主要组件？
output: Nokia HSS architecture has layered architecture consisting of a Dispatching (Load Balancing) layer and Call Processing (Business Logic or Application) layer. HSS App VNFC is also known as HSSCallP; HLR App VNFC is also known as HLRCallP; Mgmt VNFC is also known as OAM. All software components with direct transport communication to peer NEs (e.g. MME, CSCF, MSC, VLR) and those interacting with supporting NEs (SDL, HSM, CMRepo, NetAct) are at Dispatching layer. Components with HSS and HLR business logic (UMS transaction processing in HSS and Call processing in HLR) are in the Call Processing layer.

instruction: CMS-8200 HSS 使用哪几种镜像和 VNFC？
output: Nokia HSS is deployed with four images: HSS Image, HLR Image, SLB Image, Mgmt VNFC Image. VNFC to image mapping: HSS App VNFC uses HSS Image; HLR App VNFC uses HLR Image; ALB VNFC uses HSS Image for HSS-only and NokiaHSS deployment, HLR image for HLR-only deployment; DLB VNFC uses HSS Image; SLB VNFC uses SLB Image; Management VNFC uses Mgmt VNFC Image.

instruction: DLB 的主要功能是什么？
output: Diameter Load Balancer (DLB) is the Diameter protocol handler that load balances incoming Diameter traffic across Application VMs. It hosts Diameter Dispatcher to manage peer connections, capabilities negotiation, message send/receive and load balance incoming traffic to different HSS Application VNFCs. DIA: the protocol handler balances the load of incoming Diameter traffic across application VMs. Diameter stack runs on DLB and not on HSS, so Diameter signaling is terminated on DLB VMs.

instruction: ALB 提供哪些主要功能？
output: The Authentication Load Balancer (ALB) provides software based authentication if SoftAuC is selected, or alternatively LB functionality if HSM box is selected. Main ALB modules: ACP (HSM batch formulation and transaction states for authentication request response correlation), ACU (HSM connection pool management and load balancing of HSM traffic), ASH (handles Auc-reencryption requests from SDL, uses ACU for reaching HSM), ACX (fetches data from HSM for auditing and reporting), LI (LI X2 records dispatching toward LIMS). SOAP dispatcher is also hosted on ALB.

instruction: SLB 的作用和运行模式是什么？
output: MAP/SIGTRAN Load Balancer (SLB) has functionality up to SCCP and load balances traffic to HLR APP VMs on internal SIGTRAN links. It is a single-entry point for SIGTRAN traffic, operates in active-active mode with two nodes, uses provisioned TPD or node-specific data, integrates with CMRepo. SLB pair has only one Signaling Point Code. It distributes traffic in HLR in provisioned ratio. Messages are routed based on TID of TCAP message. Telesys SLB provides load balancing for SS7/SIGTRAN traffic with two-node solution for High Availability.

instruction: Mgmt VNFC 的两大功能是什么？
output: Mgmt VNFC has two distinct functionalities: 1) Act as gateway for each VNFC (DLB, ALB, HSS VM) in VNF to reach CMRepo and SWRepo – every VM uses MgmtVNFC as gateway to fetch software patches and configuration; Internal OAM CIPA IP is used as gateway IP by other VMs to reach CMRepo/SWrepo. 2) Act as single integration point towards NMS (e.g. NetAct) – Active MgmtVNFC hosts CIPA IP; supports Counters, Alarms and Log data to NMS; data is sent from every VM to MgmtVNFC using Kafka.

instruction: CM Repo 在 CMS-8200 HSS 中的作用？
output: CM Repo based central configuration is a pre-requisite for Nokia HSS VNF. The CM Repo VNF operates in 1+1 cluster (master-slave) configuration. It contains configuration data for Nokia VNFs (HSS, HLR, CSCF). Nokia HSS VNFs use the CM data directly from the CM Repo through API query. It connects directly to the CM master, but if master is not reachable it connects to the slave. Nokia HSS identifies CM Server using IP address(es) or FQDN. The role of TIAMS server in bare metal is taken over by SW repository for storing VM software images.

instruction: Nokia HSS 的 overload 机制如何工作？
output: Overload mechanism comprises measurements (CPU-load, queue size), threshold settings and load level determination. Overload handling process monitors VM resource utilization and calculates VM load level, shares VM load status to application process instances. Nokia HSS supports a Centralized Overload Manager which collects load on each VM and calculates the VNF load, broadcasts to all Call Processing VMs so designated application processes react per configured overload policy. If VNF overload is detected by Master OLH, alarms 15597889, 15597888, 15597889, 15597890, 15597899, 15597900, 15597901, 15597891, 15597902 are raised.

instruction: Traffic re-balancing 在什么情况下触发？
output: It is required to rebalance traffic across Call Processing VMs when one or more VMs utilization goes beyond allowed deviation (e.g. 5%) from VNF average. This is determined by elected master OLH process running on primary Load Balancer. Master OLH can derive processing capacity from VM resource utilization measured by each OLH instance and rebalance traffic to align with individual VM processing capacity. Rebalancing uses deviation from mean: e.g. when average resource utilization > 70 and deviation >= 5%, 5% traffic reduction from overloaded VM and distribute equally to remaining VMs.

instruction: SOAP dispatcher 部署在哪里？端口是什么？
output: SOAP dispatcher is hosted on ALB VM and acts as Single Point of Entry (SPOE) for SOAP traffic towards Nokia HSS. The entry points are: HSS: 50300 and HLR: 40300. ALB VM's role is extended to host the SOAP dispatcher; Call Processing layer constitutes HSS and HLR VMs. SOAP dispatcher provides solution to configure ALB nodes as end points of NTF instead of AppVm. ALB nodes route the traffic to respective AppVm over Internode Connection Manager (ICM). For HSS VNF, primary and secondary ALB behave in active-active mode.

instruction: TID based routing 是什么？
output: While operating as a Proxy solution with Point Code sharing, MACH7-HA Load Balancer supports load distribution to ASPs while maintaining transaction stickiness. ASPs are provisioned with TCAP transaction prefix representing range of transaction IDs (TID). MACH7 uses this to route subsequent ingress messages (CONTINUE, END, ABORT) of the ongoing transaction to correct ASP. When message for ongoing transaction is received at MACH7 in SG mode from network, it looks for TID prefix (e.g. TID label 0x1F1800FF, prefix value 0xFF from bit 24 to 31). Specified ASP must generate all TIDs with provisioned prefix so MACH7 can route correctly.

instruction: MAP overload 的 trigger 参数有哪些？
output: Operator can configure for each trigger: Onset value (determines first overload level, e.g. buffer %); Level difference (onset for next overload level); Maximum message count (for message rate trigger, after how many messages sampling is done); Guard timer (for countering overload state when no incoming messages to change state); Control ratio (controls messages to be discarded during overload, three ratios for level 1, 2, 3); Abatement ratio (determines condition for clearing overload, expressed in percentage). Discard Rate = (100 * overload level * level difference) ÷ (overload onset value + (overload level * level difference)); Message drop rate = (control ratio * discard rate) ÷ 100.

instruction: SoftAuC 和 HSM box 的区别？
output: SoftAuC is the Software module integrated into the Application VM (HLR / HSS). Software module integrated into the HLR VM is used commonly by HLR and CMS-8200 HSS VM (External AuC/HLR). Part of the AuC is located in a separated external HW (HSM box). In HSM method, the used HW components is the same as for bare metal systems (HSM Box, Utimaco CryptoServer). ALB provides software based authentication if SoftAuC is selected, or LB functionality if HSM box is selected. HSM LAN1 and LAN 2 are used by ALB Dispatcher for HSM connectivity and should be enabled only for HSM based authentication, not required if SoftAuC is enabled.

instruction: CMS-8200 HSS 有哪些部署模式？
output: Based on deployment types the system can contain HSS VM only, HLR VM only or the combination of HSS and HLR VMs (Combined-mode VNF). HSS-only VM variant contains DLB; HLR-only VM variant contains SLB; Combined HSS and HLR system uses both DLB and SLB; ALB is an obligatory part of all deployment variations. It is responsible for protocol interaction with partner elements and to balance the traffic load received on the Call Processor VM.

instruction: HLR VM 支持哪些关键功能？
output: NT HLR application VM (vHLR) supports: HLR business logic; SOAP (dispatches SOAP triggers based on message type to given application VM, maintains HTTP transaction information); LDAP (LDAP request dispatching unit toward databases based on configured load balancing model). Multiple HLR VMs are at the backend of load-balancing SLB nodes. The interface between SLB and NT HLR FE VMs is SIGTRAN. New interface between HLR VM and ALB VM uses ICM communication. SCTP, SIGTRAN and Diameter stacks run on the VM.

instruction: CMS-8200 HSS 技术描述中提到了哪些相关文档？
output: The following documents are associated with the CMS-8200 HSS Technical Description: CMS-8200 HSS Feature Description, Operating CMS-8200 HSS, Sh Interface Description, Cx Interface Description, Zh Interface Description, S6 Interface Description, SWx Interface Description, Wx Interface Description, Gq Interface Description. For more information on overall IMS description, refer to IMS System Description.

instruction: IMS 支持哪些接入和业务？
output: IMS supports a wide range of access technologies including xDSL, cable, WLAN, GPRS, EDGE, and UMTS-PS. Internal IMS signaling is realized by Diameter, SIP and SDP over IP. Both IPv4 and IPv6 are supported. Besides VoIP and video over IP, IMS supports: legacy services (IN services, online/offline charging, IP Centrex, VPN); new peer-to-peer services (voice communication, 2-party chat, peer-to-peer messaging); server-based services (picture share, PoC, presence); interworking between different IP domains and FMC with CS/PSTN.

instruction: HSSd 和 HSS-FE 的关系？
output: The document describes HSSd architecture and HSS-FE block diagram. HSS and HLR Front-Ends (FEs) are transaction-based network elements and maintain no state information about subscriber or sessions. They are developed, released, and deployed as standalone NEs. FEs support authentication and authorization for IOT, 4G, IMS, 3G, 2G, CS subscribers. The subscriber data is stored in a common subscriber profile repository outside FEs. Both NEs authenticate and authorize subscribers from different networks and share common functionalities related to subscriber profile access, profile change notifications, and subscriber authentication.

instruction: SLB 的 system states 和 HA 机制？
output: SLB system states are DOWN, UP, CONFIGURED, READY, and ACTIVE. NetAct is in Active-Active mode. OAM operation can be done on any node; other node is synced automatically in real time. Traffic handling module is multi-threaded; CPU and disk are constantly monitored with relevant alarms. Cluster isolation is automatically detected via network interface; as recovery, Node 3 is restarted. Rolling Software Upgrade is available for zero downtime. Both EMS are active for provisioning (Active-Active). During cluster isolation only Node 2 remains active and Node 3 stays down. Alarm logs and Statistics are not synced; during complete isolation, OAM operation should be done on Node 2.
